pages.title: µśÄ: read the area
pages.text: Position: scouting. µśÄ highlights useful or dangerous information around you, with higher staves increasing range and detail.$(br2)It is best before entering a cave, structure, or arena.

pages.title: µśÄ tiers
pages.text: Wood 30s, interactive blocks glimmer$(br)Stone 45s, trap clues$(br)Copper 60s, ore/container direction$(br)Iron 90s, mechanisms, danger blocks, rare ore$(br)Gold 45s, high hint density but short$(br)Diamond 120s, farther outlines$(br)Netherite 180s, largest range

pages.title: Ķäē: life pulse
pages.text: Position: reads nearby life pressure. Ķäē tells you where living threats are and how strong the area feels.$(br2)Use it before committing to Õģź, Õó©, or an unknown cave turn.

pages.title: Ķäē tiers
pages.text: Wood 20s, very close life strength$(br)Stone 30s, rough direction$(br)Copper 45s, normal vs strong enemy$(br)Iron 60s, mob-group density$(br)Gold 30s, high precision but short$(br)Diamond 90s, strong enemy direction/distance band$(br)Netherite 120s, strong enemy/friendly/hunted target

pages.title: ķŁä: reclaim death loss
pages.text: Position: partial recovery after death. ķŁä does not simply restore 80% of everything.$(br2)It groups lost stacks by item identity first. 48 planks are one group, 12 torches another, one damaged sword another.

pages.title: ķŁä grouping
pages.text: First, it rolls which item groups can return. Then each returned group rolls how much of that group returns.$(br2)Enchants, durability, and components can split tools into separate groups. Odd counts round up when halved by failure rules.

pages.title: ķŁä tiers
pages.text: Wood returns 20% groups, 20% per group, 20% XP$(br)Stone 30/30/30$(br)Copper 40/40/40$(br)Iron 50/50/50$(br)Gold 60/60/60$(br)Diamond 70/70/70$(br)Netherite 80/80/80
